title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page analyzes the relationship between life expectancy and population in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

plt.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.5)
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Population")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()







Figure 1: Life expectancy vs population





As shown in Figure 1, population size alone does not determine life expectancy.

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page analyzes the relationship between life expectancy and GDP per capita in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.5)
plt.xscale("log")
plt.xlabel("GDP per capita (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()







Figure 1: Life expectancy vs GDP per capita





As shown in Figure 1, countries with higher GDP per capita tend to have higher life expectancy.

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page tracks how life expectancy changes over time for one country.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

country_name = "United States"  # change if needed
c = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")

print("Selected country:", country_name)
print("Rows:", c.shape[0])

plt.plot(c["year"], c["life_expectancy"], marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title(f"Life Expectancy Over Time: {country_name}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Selected country: United States
Rows: 12








Figure 1: Life expectancy over time for a selected country





This plot shows the trend in life expectancy for the selected country.

title: Gapminder Dashboard
text: The Gapminder dataset contains life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population data for countries from 1952 to 2007.

title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))
